extra_docs.extra_documentation: When used in assembly contexts, this method can return a Member object representing the selected assembly component.
If the configuration name specified in ExistingConfigName is empty or does not exist, the current configuration of the component is used.

When editing a mate without repairing missing entities, the selection order differs from the repair scenario: first select the two items that are mated (for example, two faces, edge and face, and so on) with a selection mark of 1, then select the mate feature to be edited last as the third selection.

In the predecessor method CopyWithMates (SOLIDWORKS 2010), the FlipAlignment parameter was documented as valid for distance and angle mates only, whereas the current method does not specify this restriction for FlipAlignment.
When adding multiple instances of the same component to the assembly, include the component's file name in the Names array for each instance you want to add.
See example: Cut Body in Half using Macro Feature (VBA) for usage demonstration.
See 'Accessing Selections that Define Features' for additional details on using this property.
When Option is set to swUserDecision and the user answers No to the rebuild dialog, the activated document is not rebuilt and swDocNeedsRebuildWarning is returned in the Errors argument.
For additional details on using this property, see Accessing Selections that Define Features.

When pre-selecting entities to mate using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2, use selection mark 8 for cam-follower faces (swMateCAMFOLLOWER), selection mark 16 for width mates with tab selections (swMateWIDTH), and selection mark 1 for all other mate types. If nothing is preselected, the error status swAddMateError_IncorrectSelections is returned and no mate is created.

The LockRotation option allows locking the rotation of components in the mate. When using pre-selection, selection marks follow the pattern: 8 for swMateType_e.swMateCAMFOLLOWER, 16 for swMateType_e.swMateWIDTH, and 1 for all other swMateType_e options.

To specify a distance mate without limits, set the absolute upper and lower limit values equal to the distance value. When Flip is set to true for distance or angle mates (swMateType_e.swMateDISTANCE or swMateType_e.swMateANGLE), the components move to the other possible mate position if the mate is applied to the closest position meeting the mate condition. The ForPositioningOnly option allows positioning components according to the mating relationship without creating and returning a mate feature.

This method does not display the Interference Detection PropertyManager, unlike the original obsolete ToolsCheckInterference method which opened the PropertyManager UI. A VBA example titled 'Check Interference (VBA)' is available demonstrating usage of this functionality.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes information about the type of bend allowance as defined in swBendAllowanceTypes_e.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a numeric bend allowance value.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a K-Factor value.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object may reference a bend table file path.
The custom bend allowance type is defined by the swBendAllowanceTypes_e enumeration.
The ICustomBendAllowance object can specify a bend table file path.
The ICustomBendAllowance object can specify a K-Factor value.
The ICustomBendAllowance object can specify a numeric bend allowance value.
The FaceList parameter accepts Face objects, despite being typed as System.object in the signature.
The FaceList parameter should be an array of Face2 objects (passed as System.object).
The FaceList parameter should contain an array of faces, with the array size corresponding to the number of faces to be deleted.

All specified faces must belong to this temporary body. When using dependent loop processing (option 0 in the deprecated method), if extending faces does not yield a solution, SOLIDWORKS tries to shrink the faces as a fallback. When using independent loop processing with face growing (option 1), the method grows the parent faces around the hole to cover it. When using independent loop processing with surface attachment (option 2), the method finds a surface in which all edges of a hole lie and attaches this to a new face covering the hole.

When working with bodies in an assembly, the second body must be transformed in its coordinate space so that it is positioned the same with respect to the first body as it is in assembly space. This clarifies the purpose of the transformation required before calling this method.

When creating derived pattern features from selected components and pattern features, use IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 with ordered selection: seed component with Mark=1 and pattern feature with Mark=6.

For mirror components created via IMirrorComponentFeatureData, you can specify component naming using prefix or suffix modifiers defined by swMirrorComponentNameModifier_e, and optionally copy custom properties from the selected components to the mirrored components.

When mirroring components, you can create derived configurations instead of new part files by setting CreateDerivedConfigurations to true. This is valid only when MirroredComponentFilenames is provided and NameModifierType is swMirrorComponentName_Custom. You can also break links between mirror parts and original parts, preserve or flip Z-axis orientation in mirror parts, and control whether to orient mirror components about center of mass or bounding box center.
The SyncFlexibleSubAssemblies option controls whether to synchronize the movement of flexible subassembly components with the parent component in mirrored instances.

To create a sheet metal Normal Cut feature using ISMNormalCutFeatureData2, follow this sequence: (1) AddSMNormalCut, (2) AddSMNormalCutType, (3) SelectByID2 with Mark=1 for cut-extrude faces, Mark=2 for groups of faces, Mark=4 for offset plane if not using extent, Mark=8 for direction if using extent, (4) FinishSMNormalCut, (5) optionally modify ISMNormalCutFeatureData::Offset to adjust the profile. The AutoPropagation option controls automatic propagation, Optimize controls geometry optimization, and BExtent determines whether to maximize cutout using top/bottom intersection profiles or use an offset plane.

To create a variable pattern feature (which uses a table to store dimensions and values of pattern instances), call IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceOverride before calling CreateFeature with IDimPatternFeatureData to create an advanced variable pattern feature.

Chain pattern features have three types: distance, distance linkage, and connected linkage. Selection marks must be used as follows: Path (sketch/edge) = Mark 2; Chain Group 1 Component = Mark 1; Chain Group 1 Path Link 1 = Mark 256; Chain Group 1 Path Link 2 = Mark 512 (for linkage types only); Chain Group 1 Path Alignment Plane = Mark 16384; Chain Group 2 Component = Mark 2048 (connected linkage only); Chain Group 2 Path Link 1 = Mark 4096 (connected linkage only); Chain Group 2 Path Link 2 = Mark 8192 (connected linkage only); Chain Group 2 Path Alignment Plane = Mark 32768 (connected linkage only); Face normal alignment plane = Mark 1024 (only if path is a sketch line). To set equal spacing between chain pattern instances after creation, edit the chain pattern feature.

For circular pattern features, if VaryInstance is true to vary dimensions or spacing of individual pattern instances, you must call either IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceIncrement or IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceOverride before calling CreateFeature with ICircularPatternFeatureData, depending on the desired variation type (Increment or Override). This is valid only when GeometryPattern is false.

When using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 for circular patterns with ordered selection: for features, use Mark=1 for direction axis and Mark=4 for features to pattern; for components, use Mark=1 for components to pattern and Mark=2 for direction axis. When using IFeature::Select2 directly: for features, use Mark=1 for axis and Mark=4 for features; for components, use ISelectData::Mark and IComponent2::Select3 with Mark=1 for components and Mark=2 for axis. The SyncSubAssemblies option controls whether to move components in patterned instances when components are moved in the seed flexible subassembly. Bidirectional circular patterns can be created using BDir2, BSymmetric, Number2, Spacing2, DName2, and EqualSpacing2 parameters.

For fill pattern features created via IFillPatternFeatureData, use these selection marks with IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2: Mark=1 for fill boundary (sketch, face, planar curve on face, or coplanar faces); Mark=2 for direction reference (e.g., edge or axis); Mark=3 for location of pattern seed instance (vertex or sketch point, or SOLIDWORKS chooses center if not selected); Mark=4 for features to pattern; Mark=5 for faces to pattern; Mark=6 for bodies to pattern. The PatternLayoutType determines the layout (Perforation, Circular, Polygon, or Square). For all layouts except Perforation, the pattern is created by positioning a seed instance within the fill boundary and copying in concentric loops. Various seed cut types can be created (Circle, Diamond, Square, Polygon) with corresponding geometric parameters (Diameter, Dimension, Diagonal, OuterRadius, InnerRadius, Rotation, CreateSeedCutPolygonSides).

For linear pattern features, if VaryInstance is true to vary dimensions and spacing of individual pattern instances (valid only when GeometryPattern is false), you must call either IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceIncrement or IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceOverride before calling CreateFeature with ILinearPatternFeatureData or ILocalLinearPatternFeatureData, depending on the variation type desired (Increment or Override). When using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 with ordered selection: for features, use Mark=1 and Mark=2 for directions and Mark=4 for features to pattern; for components, use Mark=1 for components to pattern and Mark=2 and Mark=4 for directions. When directly selecting features or components, use IFeature::Select2 for features or ISelectData::Mark with IComponent2::Select3 for components.

When creating linear patterns with VaryInstance set to true, you must call either IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceIncrement or IFeatureManager::InsertVaryInstanceOverride before calling the pattern creation method to specify how individual pattern instances should vary.

Selection marks for linear patterns: Use mark 1 for Direction 1, mark 2 for Direction 2, mark 4 for features to pattern, mark 256 for solid bodies to pattern, mark 1 for components to pattern (with marks 2 and 4 for component directions), mark 2097152 for offset references, and mark 8388608 for seed instance references when not using centroids. When using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2, ordered selection of features and components is required.

Selection marks for local curve-driven patterns: Use mark 1 for components to pattern, mark 2 for curve/edge/sketch/sketch entity for Direction 1, mark 4 for Direction 2, mark 32 for reference points (which must be vertices if using swLocalSketchPatternSelectedPoint), and mark 64 for faces on which 3D curves lie.

Selection marks for local sketch-driven patterns: Use mark 1 for components to pattern, mark 16 for the sketch defining the pattern, and mark 32 for reference points (which must be vertices if using swLocalSketchPatternSelectedPoint).

Before creating sketch-driven patterns, select required entities using proper selection marks: mark 4 for features, mark 32 for points, mark 64 for sketches, mark 128 for faces, and mark 256 for bodies.

When creating derived patterns (also called pattern driven pattern features), use IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 to select seed components with mark 1 and the pattern feature with mark 2 in any order.
When UseAutoSelect is set to false and bodies are selected using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 or other select methods, use selection mark 8 for the bodies.

Before creating sheet metal edge flanges, call IModelDoc2::InsertSketchForEdgeFlange and create a profile for the flange. After creating the profile, call the feature creation method to create the flange.

For table-driven patterns, coordinates for input points must be specified in system units, either in a .sldptab or .txt file, or directly in a PointVar array. Because each point has two coordinates (x, y), the size of PointVar is (2 x number_of_points). Selection marks required: 4 = Seed feature, 16 = Coordinate system, 32 = Reference point, 128 = Seed face, 256 = Seed body. If UseCentroid is false, a reference point must be specified.

For sheet metal gusset features, pre-select entities using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 before creating the feature. To specify gusset legs: select two flat faces of a bend, or two flat faces adjacent to a cylindrical bend, or one cylindrical bend face, or one flat face and one non-planar face adjacent to a toroidal bend, or one toroidal bend face. Optionally orient the gusset section plane by selecting one linear edge or sketch segment perpendicular to the section plane (if omitted, the bend line adjacent to the gusset legs is used). Optionally position the gusset section plane by selecting one vertex, sketch point, or reference point (if omitted, BOffset must be true with a specified DOffset for an assumed reference point on the sheet metal bend end for the first gusset, or on the edge of the last inserted gusset for subsequent insertions). For ProfDimType = 1, to dimension using both legs specify DLength of d1 leg, DHeight of d2 leg, and BUseAngle = false; to dimension using d1 leg and angle specify DLength, DAngle of angle formed by gusset intersecting d1 leg, and BUseAngle = true; to dimension using d2 leg and angle specify DHeight, DAngle of angle formed by gusset intersecting d2 leg, and BUseAngle = true. BFlipSides = true swaps d1 and d2 legs during dimensioning.
For curve-driven patterns, Spacing1 and Spacing2 parameters are specified in radians.
ToolBody parameter expects a Body object.

FaceList2 is the second face list (parallel to FaceList1) containing Face2 objects that correspond to matching faces in the target body. The current parameter description incorrectly states 'Number of matching faces' which duplicates NumOfMatchingFaces.
The return value is specifically an array of Component2 objects.
Return Value: True if the selections are successfully accessed, false if not

For mirror component features (swFmMirrorComponent): ComponentOrientations is valid only if ComponentsToInstance is not null. OrientAboutCenterOfMass controls whether to orient mirror components about center of mass (true) or bounding box center (false). When CreateDerivedConfigurations is true, MirroredComponentFilenames contains names for new configurations and NameModifierType must be swMirrorComponentName_Custom. ImportOptions uses swMirrorPartOptions_e enumeration for mirror transfer options. BreakLinks controls whether to break links between mirror parts and original parts. PreserveZAxis controls whether to mirror or flip the Z-axis orientation in the mirror part.
For mirror component features (swFmMirrorComponent): SyncFlexibleSubAssemblies parameter controls whether to synchronize the movement of flexible subassembly components with the parent component.

For convert solid to sheet metal features (swFmSolidToSheetMetal): OverlapType for all rips uses numeric values: 1=Open butt, 2=Overlap, 3=Underlap. OverlapRatio specifies the overlap ratio for all rips. KeepBody controls whether to keep bodies during conversion. Consult SOLIDWORKS Help for additional information about converting to sheet metal.

For library form tool features (swFmFormToolInstance): Before calling CreateFeature, select either a face (for single instance placement) or a 2D sketch containing points (for multiple instance placement at each point). Path specifies the pathname of the forming tool part file in the Design Library. OverrideDoc controls whether to override document settings in Tools > Options > Document Properties > Sheet Metal. ShowPunch, ShowProfile, and ShowCenter control display options when the part is flattened and are valid only if OverrideDoc is true. LinkToPart controls whether to link the forming tool feature to its part in the Design Library.

For bounding box features (swFmBoundingBox): If BBoxType is set to swBoundingBoxType_CustomPlane, select a face or plane using IModelDocExtension::SelectByRay with TypeWanted set to swSelFACES before calling CreateFeature. To display or hide the bounding box sketch, call IModelDoc2::SetUserPreferenceToggle to set swViewDispGlobalBBox to true or false. After creating the bounding box, use IModelDoc2::ClearSelection2 to clear the selection made when the bounding box is created. Status returns swGlobalBoundingBoxResult_e values. IncludeHiddenBodies and IncludeSurfaceBodies control which bodies are included in the bounding box calculation.

For ground plane features (swFmGroundPlane): Before calling CreateFeature, select the entity to use as the ground plane using IModelDocExtension::SelectByRay or IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2. Ground planes are used in plant assemblies as reference geometry when inserting published assets at each level. Call IAssemblyDoc::ActivateGroundPlane to activate the ground plane where you want to insert a published asset; the asset's ground face snaps to the assembly's activated ground plane. Components with magnetic mates snap only to components on the active ground plane. Only one ground plane can be active at a time in each assembly configuration. See Large Assemblies > Facility Layout in SOLIDWORKS Help for more information.

For sheet metal base flange features (swFmBaseFlange): When UseAutoSelect is set to false and bodies are selected using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 or other select methods, use selection mark 8 for the bodies. Merge controls whether to connect bodies into a single sheet metal body. UseFeatScope controls whether to use selected bodies (true) or all bodies (false).

For sheet metal edge flange features (swFmEdgeFlange): Before calling CreateFeature, call IModelDoc2::InsertSketchForEdgeFlange and create a profile for the flange. After creating the profile, call CreateFeature to create the flange. FlangeEdges is an array of edges to which to apply the flange. SketchFeats is an array of sketches to use for the flange. BooleanOptions uses swInsertEdgeFlangeOptions_e enumeration. BendPosition uses swFlangePositionTypes_e enumeration. ReliefType uses swSheetMetalReliefTypes_e enumeration. FlangeSharpType uses swFlangeDimTypes_e enumeration. If CustomBendAllowance is NULL, the parent bend's bend allowance is used.

For sheet metal gusset features (swFmSMGusset): Before calling CreateFeature, pass references in arrays or pre-select using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2. To specify gusset legs: select two flat faces of a bend, two flat faces adjacent to a cylindrical bend face, one cylindrical bend face, one flat face and one non-planar face adjacent to a toroidal bend, or one toroidal bend face. To orient the gusset section plane (optional): select one linear edge or sketch segment perpendicular to the gusset section plane; if not selected, the bend line adjacent to gusset legs is used. To position the gusset section plane (optional): select one vertex, sketch point, or reference point; if not selected, set BOffset to true and specify DOffset for an assumed reference point (on end of sheet metal bend for first gusset, or on edge of last inserted gusset for subsequent insertions). ArrayOfFaces contains one or two IFace2 objects. ArrayRefLines contains one linear IEdge or ISketchSegment. ArrayRefPoints contains one IVertex, ISketchPoint, or IRefPoint. If ProfDimType equals 1 (profile dimensioning), dimensioning options depend on which legs and angles are specified with DLength, DHeight, DAngle, BUseAngle, and BFlipSides parameters.

For sheet metal gusset features (swFmSMGusset): BOverrideDoc controls whether to override document flat pattern properties. BShowProfile controls whether to display the gusset profile when flattened (valid only if BOverrideDoc is true). BShowCenter controls whether to display center marks when flattened (valid only if BOverrideDoc is true).

For projection curve features (swFmRefCurve): The Reverse parameter controls the direction in which the curve is projected. This is necessary only when the selected face wraps around the plane of the curve. For example, if the sketch being projected is surrounded by a cylindrical face, two possible projections exist. The reverse argument switches the direction based on the normal vector of the sketch; the default direction is along the sketch normal.

When creating projection curve features (swFmRefCurve), note that the legacy method supported a Reverse parameter to control projection direction. The returned feature data object should be configured accordingly and used with CreateFeature to obtain the resulting Feature object.

When initializing sheet metal base flange feature data (swFmBaseFlange), typical parameters include: Thickness, ThickenDir (direction to thicken), Radius (bend radius), ExtrudeDist1 and ExtrudeDist2 (extrusion distances), FlipExtruDir (flip extrusion direction), EndCondition1 and EndCondition2 (end condition types), and DirToUse (direction to use for the feature).
Example code demonstrating usage is available for C#, VB.NET, and VBA (Create and Modify Closed Corner Feature examples).
Call this method before calling IComponent2::GetRenderMaterials2 to get the size of the array for that method.
This method supports lightweight components.
When a component is excluded from the BOM, the name of the component in the FeatureManager design tree changes; (Excluded from BOM) is appended.

When NumberOfDerivatives=1, the return array contains 6 doubles in the following order: retval[0]=x location, retval[1]=y location, retval[2]=z location, retval[3]=x tangent vector, retval[4]=y tangent vector, retval[5]=z tangent vector. This corresponds to the behavior of the superseded Evaluate method.
You can only set this property for the top-level CommandGroup.
The return value is System.object and must be cast to a float array before use.

For practical usage examples, see: Get Edge Curve Parameterization (VBA), Get intersecting Faces (VBA), Get Length of Edge (VBA), Get Reference Curve Information (VBA), and Get Start and End Points of Spline (VBA).

How components appear in the BOM report is mainly controlled by the display format of the BOM, though this property also affects the output format. When child components are not shown (e.g., when set to Hide), the BOM includes only the subassembly itself, not its component parts, even if the BOM report otherwise requests that all parts be displayed.

Alternative to step 1 in traversal order: You can use IConfigurationManager::ActiveConfiguration instead of IModelDoc2::GetConfigurationByName to obtain the configuration before calling GetRootComponent3, particularly when working with the currently active configuration.

Options parameter details: swConfigOption_SuppressByDefault means newly added features and mates in this configuration will be suppressed; swConfigOption_HideByDefault means newly added components will be hidden; swConfigOption_MinFeatureManager means newly added components will only display their component name in the FeatureManager design tree, not their individual features. Additional usage examples include: Set Dimensions to Mid-Tolerance (VBA) and Add Derived Configurations (VBA).

The superseded AddExplodeStep method supported two parameters not present in AddExplodeStep2: RigidSubassembly (to explode an entire subassembly versus just the selected component) and ExplodeRelated (to explode related components together). If you require this functionality, you may need to select multiple components manually or use alternative approaches when calling AddExplodeStep2.
The PropNames and PropValues arrays contain string values, while the PropTypes array contains integer values corresponding to swCustomInfoType_e enumeration members.
The returned coordinate values and derivative vectors are in meters.

The curve is represented as a series of segments, each having the form: p(t) = (w0*a0 + w1*a1*t + w2*a2*t**2 + w3*a3*t**3) / (w0 + w1*t + w2*t**2 + w3*t**3), where w is the weight coefficient and a is the polynomial coefficient. For rational curves, the dimension is 4 (x, y, z, w); for non-rational curves, the dimension is 3 (x, y, z). Coefficients for each segment are ordered from constant term to highest degree term (e.g., ConstantX, ConstantY, ConstantZ, AX, AY, AZ, BX, BY, BZ).

The deprecated GetCustomProperties method returned the count of custom properties as an integer and populated three out-parameter arrays with property names (strings), values (strings), and types (integers, though declared as System.object in the method signature).
The deprecated GetCustomPropertiesCount method returned the count of custom properties as an integer.

For cross-language compatibility, when modifying a feature in a Part, the Component argument can be specified as Nothing (VB.NET) or null (C#). Example usage available in: Get and Set Seed Components (C#/VB.NET/VBA) and Get Number of Instances Skipped in Driving Feature (C#/VB.NET/VBA).
Radial and diametric dimensions both return swRadialDimension. To determine how this radial dimension is currently displayed, use IDisplayDimension::Diametric and IDisplayDimension::DisplayAsLinear.

Note: Return values changed from the deprecated SetUnits method. SetUnits returned -2 and -1 for errors, whereas SetUnits2 returns 2 and 1 respectively. Code migrating from SetUnits must update error-checking logic accordingly.
The objects in the returned array are Face2 instances.
IModelDoc2::GraphicsRedraw2 can also be used to see the effects of changing tolerance values, as was recommended in earlier API versions.
Lower text content and lower text are only effective when the balloon style is set to swBS_SplitCirc (split circle). See SOLIDWORKS Help for additional details about autoballoons.

To get or set document default values for balloon properties programmatically, use IModelDocExtension::GetUserPreferenceInteger and IModelDocExtension::SetUserPreferenceInteger with the following preference constants: swDetailingAutoBalloonLayout for Layout, swDetailingBOMBalloonStyle for Style, swDetailingBOMBalloonFit or swDetailingBOMStackedBalloonFit for Size, swDetailingBOMUpperText for UpperTextContent, and swDetailingBOMLowerText for LowerTextContent. All use swDetailingNoOptionSpecified for the option parameter.
This method allows you to get only the balloons just created by returning them in the array, rather than having to query all balloons in the document.
The superseded CreateText method returned a boolean indicating success or failure, whereas CreateText2 returns the created Note object directly.
The block definition can include the following entity types from the sketch: SketchSegment objects, SketchPoint objects, Note objects, DisplayDimension objects, and BlockInstance objects.
The array parameters P0, P1, P2, P3, P4, and TextPoint are double arrays passed by reference.
The MathPoint objects in the Points parameter represent the leader points (vertices along the multi-jog leader path).
This method is equivalent to interactively getting document properties in the SOLIDWORKS product. Available since SOLIDWORKS 2001Plus FCS, Revision Number 10.0.

Example implementations are available demonstrating usage scenarios: getting the component for a selected entity, getting the component from a feature, getting the component via a display dimension, and getting BOM balloon properties in VBA, C#, and VB.NET.
The return value is a View object representing the newly created detail view.

The curve parameters include a SenseFlag that indicates whether the curve and edge are in the same direction. If SenseFlag is True, the curve and edge are in the same direction. If SenseFlag is False, the curve and edge are in opposite directions; in this case, the start parameter corresponds to the end of the edge and the end parameter corresponds to the start of the edge. The start parameter is always smaller than the end parameter. When curve and edge are in opposite directions, the start and end parameters are along the negative portion of the parameter space. For example, if the edge start parameter is 10 and end parameter is 5, the returned range is -10 to -5. To correct for the edge, swap the start and end parameters and negate both values. If the curve is closed and starts and ends at the same point, then StartUParam and EndUParam are a period apart.
Point values returned in the array are specified in meters.
An additional example demonstrating basic sheet setup without zones is available: Change Setup of Drawing Sheet (VBA).
When setting a face reference, the PDisp parameter should be a Face2 object.
When setting a vertex reference, the PDisp parameter should be a Vertex object, although it is declared as System.object.

Predecessor available since SOLIDWORKS 2001Plus FCS, Revision Number 10.0 (current version available since 2009). Return value in predecessor documented as indicating success or failure of the toggle operation, rather than the resulting state.
Additional usage examples from predecessor: Hide or Show All Types (VBA) and Ignore Feature Colors (VBA).
Can be used with swUserPreferenceToggle_e.swGridDisplay to control grid display in drawings.
Can be used with swDisplayFeatureDimensions enumeration value to control visibility of feature dimensions.
Print setup functionality may require use of SetUserPreferenceDoubleValue and SetUserPreferenceIntegerValue methods in addition to SetUserPreferenceToggle.
When the end condition reference is a face, the return value is a Face2 object.
When the end condition reference is a vertex, the return value is a Vertex object.

The return value is specifically an array of doubles. For the ith arc (zero-indexed), the values are accessed as: retval[11 * i + 0] through retval[11 * i + 10], where each arc now contains 11 values (9 coordinates plus 2 boolean flags).
The returned silhouette edges are not added to the face and cannot be found using IGetEdges. Before calling this method, use IGetSilhoutteEdgeCount to determine the required array size.
The parameters BoxLowIn and BoxHighIn are arrays of doubles passed by reference.
The predecessor property IFaceHatch.Scale was get-only; its set accessor was not implemented. This property (Scale2) supersedes it and provides a functional setter.

Return value details: If this method returns False, you may have specified invalid properties in the feature data modifier object and the feature definition was not modified (the feature may have rolled back to its previous state). If it returns True, the feature was updated, but note that True is returned even if the feature data modifier object does not include any changed properties or references; some feature data modifier objects reject invalid changes before this method is called. For some feature modifications, this method applies the new properties and returns True but may still result in a new rebuild error on the feature. In either case, this method may leave the feature in an unpredictable state due to errors. After calling this method, call IFeature::GetErrorCode2 to ascertain whether the feature has rebuild errors. If so, try again with valid feature data. To correct rebuild errors, call IModelDoc2::ForceRebuild3. You can also move the rollback bar in the FeatureManager design tree to before and after the feature to correct its state. You should carefully read specific feature data interface documentation to learn how to correctly specify feature data properties and how this method works with specific features.
The Material_values array should contain double-type values.

The return value semantics differ from the deprecated EditFreeze method. In EditFreeze, 0 indicated failure (did not move) and non-zero indicated success (did move). In EditFreeze2, the values are reversed: 1 indicates failure and 0 indicates success.
See example: Change Imported File Associated with an Imported Feature (VBA), which demonstrates usage of this method.
If no entity is selected for the direction edge (selection mark 16), the direction of extrusion defaults to normal to the selected extrusion surface.

In the superseded InsertCosmeticThread2 method, the Depth parameter represented the diameter of the cosmetic thread, and the Length parameter represented the length. In InsertCosmeticThread3, these have been replaced by separate Diameter and Depth parameters with distinct meanings, and the end condition is controlled via the EndType parameter.
Alternative method for adding a hole at multiple locations: preselect the sketch points and do not specify any selection marks.

Either select the mate references interactively or select them programmatically using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 with marks of 1, 2, and 4. If the mate references are interactively selected, then specify null for PrimaryReferenceEntity, SecondaryReferenceEntity, and TertiaryReferenceEntity.

When creating a boundary surface feature (as opposed to a boundary feature), use different selection marks with IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2. For boundary surface features, use Direction 1: 1 and Direction 2: 2 for sketch curves. The selection marks listed in the main documentation apply to boundary features but not boundary surface features.
When using cut or cavity features that result in multiple bodies, you cannot select to keep all of the resulting bodies or one or more selected bodies.
For thin revolve features, a constant thickness is given to the profile when revolving.
Array parameters Radii and SetBackDistances contain double values.

Array parameters Radii, SetBackDistances, and PointRadiusArray contain double values. In unmanaged code, these arrays have associated count parameters (NRadii, SetbackDistCount, PointCount) that specify the array sizes.
When working with mirrored solid bodies, the IMirrorSolidFeatureData Interface can be used to access and modify the mirror solid feature data.
The icon files should include both button icons and separator images for this flyout.

When using the old Freeform algorithm (Nsided=false), the selected face must have exactly four sides. The boundary continuity values that were previously inline parameters are now set via IFeatureManager::SetFreeformBoundaryContinuity before calling this method, where: -1=Movable, 0=Contact, 2=Tangent, 3=Curvature.
The return value is an array of Component2 objects.
The return value is of type System.object but represents an Entity object. Cast the return value to Entity to access its properties and methods.
In earlier versions, IFeature::IModifyDefinition2 was also documented as an alternative to IFeature::ModifyDefinition for restoring the rollback state after modifying the feature.
Code examples demonstrating usage are available for C#, VB.NET, and VBA with titles Get and Set Seed Components.
Before calling this method, call ILibraryFeatureData::GetReferencesCount to determine the size of the array.
Example code samples are available: Get Linear Pattern Feature Data (C#), Get Linear Pattern Feature Data (VB.NET), and Get Linear Pattern Feature Data (VBA).

The returned System.object contains structured data including: the neutral face (Face2), separate lists of front faces and back faces, the count of front faces, the count of back faces, and the thickness. The i-prefixed method (IGetNextFaceArray) exposes these as distinct Face2 arrays with their sizes.
The returned DisplayDimension object contains a double value representing the mate dimension value.

For VB.NET users, the Component argument can be specified as Nothing (in addition to null in C#) when modifying a feature in a part. Code examples demonstrating usage are available: Get Mirror Pattern Feature Data (C#), Get Mirror Pattern Feature Data (VB.NET), and Get Mirror Pattern Feature Data (VBA).

The deprecated GetComponentName method returned the component name directly as a string. With ReferenceComponent, you must now retrieve the Component2 object and access its name through the appropriate property (e.g., Name or Name2).

The i-prefixed version IGetFirstFaceArray returns the front face list, back face list, and their respective counts as separate out parameters, providing more structured access to the face data than casting the object array.
When changing many dimensions in an active sketch, you may want to temporarily turn off automatic solving to improve performance, then re-enable it afterward.

When used with swUserPreferenceToggle_e.swSketchInference to control sketch inferencing mode: Inferencing mode is visible when creating a sketch segment and the cursor moves past another sketch item. If inferencing is turned on, a dashed line appears from the current cursor position to the inferred position on the existing sketch entity.

When used with swUserPreferenceToggle_e.swSketchInference: Setting to true enables inferencing during sketch operations (subject to other settings that may disable inferencing such as ISketchManager::AddToDB and IDrawingDoc::StartDrawing), affecting sketch entity snapping and inferring constraints to other geometry during creation. Setting to false allows faster sketching without inferencing, similar to SketchManager::AddToDB, except that this method does not disable undo operations.
The X, Y, and Z coordinate parameters are in meters.

When using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 to select entities for dimensioning, you must leave the object name argument empty. If you pass the object name to IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2, the selection routines use the object name instead of the coordinates to locate that item, causing coordinates to be ignored and resulting in unpredictable dimensioning results.
The PatternSpacing parameter is specified in radians.

The method sorts selected dimensions into groups (linear, ordinate, and angular). Within the linear group, dimensions are sorted by measured direction. Dimensions within each group are aligned together, then updated and dragged as a unit.
For VBA compatibility, the PropArray portion (dimension, order, number of control points, periodicity) consists of four integers placed in the first four doubles of the SafeArray.
See example: Sketch a Spline (VBA).

Control point coordinates must be passed in sketch space. The Z value is interpreted as 0. In certain situations, you must transform your B-curve parameters to sketch space using ISketch::ModelToSketchTransform. If the generated spline is a closed spline, then it must be flagged as periodic. Additionally, splines generated in sketches must be G1 continuous. If the data passed to this method does not generate a G1 continuous spline, then it is rejected and a spline is not created. If your data is not G1 continuous, then you must split the spline into multiple G1 segments and call this method for each segment. This method does not work with IModelDoc2::SetAddToDB or IModelDoc2::SetDisplayWhenAdded. It always adds the spline directly to the database (as if SetAddToDB(True) is in effect), and you must redraw your document window to see the entities that you added (as if SetDisplayWhenAdded(false) is in effect). To create 3D splines, see IModelDoc2::InsertCurveFilePoint and related functions. The knot array within ParamData must contain exactly (Number of Control Points + Order) elements. When Dimension=4, each control point includes a fourth component w representing weight, formatted as (x, y, z, w).

If the data passed to this method does not generate a G1 continuous spline, then it creates multiple G1 continuous spline segments. This method does not work with ISketchManager::AddToDB or ISketchManager::DisplayWhenAdded. It always adds the spline directly to the database (as if AddToDB(True) is in effect), and you must redraw your document window to see the entities that you added (as if DisplayWhenAdded(False) is in effect).
This method supports creating both 2D and 3D tangent arcs. The deprecated IModelDoc2::CreateTangentArc2 was limited to 2D arcs only, with Z coordinates always constrained to 0.

The ConfigNames parameter is an array. The I-prefixed version of this method requires an explicit Count parameter specifying the number of configurations, and ConfigNames is passed by reference as a string. In the non-i version, ConfigNames is passed as an object (typically an array) without requiring a separate count parameter.

For COM applications, you can obtain the underlying ISketchSpline object from the returned ISketchSegment by using QueryInterface. OLE applications can use the returned Dispatch pointer to call ISketchSegment or ISketchSpline APIs; Visual Basic applications interpret the pointer automatically.

For the COM interface, the total number of points in the PointData array must be passed in explicitly. For the OLE interface, the total number of points is determined automatically by taking the UBound of the PointData VARIANT and dividing by 3, so the array must be dimensioned correctly.
In 2D sketches, Z values may be included in PointData but SOLIDWORKS ignores them (rather than requiring their omission).
For assembly-specific reload operations, see also IAssemblyDoc::ReplaceComponents and IAssemblyDoc::ComponentReload2.

When used with swUserPreferenceToggle_e.swSketchInference, this method controls automatic sketch inferencing. Inferencing mode displays a dashed line from the current cursor position to the inferenced position on an existing sketch entity when creating a sketch segment and the cursor moves past another sketch entity.

When used with swUserPreferenceToggle_e.swSketchInference, setting to true enables inferencing during sketch operations (subject to other settings like AddToDB or StartDrawing that may disable it), affecting sketch entity snapping and constraint inferring to other geometry during creation. Setting to false allows faster sketching without inferencing, similar to AddToDB but without disabling undo operations.
The selected object can be an edge, silhouette, face, or vertex.
The System.object return type should be cast to Annotation for use in C#.

This method handles balloon style and size configuration only. For balloon text content, which was previously configured through the UpperTextStyle, UpperText, LowerTextStyle, and LowerText parameters of the deprecated EditBalloonProperties method, use SetBomBalloonText.

Angle values are specified in radians. The method uses the current document setting for displaying reference planes (controlled via IModelDocExtension::GetUserPreferenceToggle and SetUserPreferenceToggle with swDisplayPlanes). The created plane is not automatically selected; previously selected objects remain selected. The returned IRefPlane object can be used for subsequent operations; for COM users, methods are available via QueryInterface, while OLE users can access them directly. To select the newly created plane, use IEntity::Select4.

Offset distance values are specified in meters. The method uses the current document setting for displaying reference planes (controlled via IModelDocExtension::GetUserPreferenceToggle and SetUserPreferenceToggle with swDisplayPlanes). The created plane is not automatically selected; previously selected objects remain selected. The returned IRefPlane object can be used for subsequent operations; for COM users, methods are available via QueryInterface, while OLE users can access them directly. To select the newly created plane, use IEntity::Select4.

When multiple intersections exist between a surface, plane, and edge, the intersection index specifies which intersection point to use. If the index exceeds the number of intersection points, the last intersection point found is used. For ProjOpt: when true, projects the plane along the sketch normal toward the reference surface; when false, projects to the nearest location on the reference surface. For NormalPlane on conical surfaces: when true, finds the plane normal to the surface; when false, finds the plane tangent to the surface.

The resulting plane is not parametric. The plane normal vector is calculated using the cross product of vectors (P2 - P1) and (P3 - P1). When UseGlobal is true, the x-axis of the global coordinate system is projected onto the plane to determine the plane's x-axis direction, rotating the plane coordinate system about P1 without reorienting the plane itself. When UseGlobal is false, the plane's x-axis is aligned from P1 to P2. This method creates the plane in the model currently being edited, which differs from the original CreatePlaneFixed that always created the plane in the top-level model.
For assemblies, you can use IComponent2::GetSuppression to determine the suppression state of each component, as suppressed components are excluded from mass property calculations.
For assemblies, the returned moments of inertia (MomXX, MomYY, MomZZ, MomXY, MomZX, MomYZ) are calculated about the assembly center-of-gravity coordinate system aligned with the assembly axes.
The predecessor method GetMassProperties returned an array of size 13, with Accuracy as the 13th element. GetMassProperties2 returns an array of size 12 without the Accuracy element.

For COM applications, the returned object pointer can be used to call any APIs on the ISketchSegment interface. OLE applications can define a new ISketchSegment object using the returned Dispatch pointer. Visual Basic applications interpret the pointer automatically, allowing direct calls to ISketchSegment functions.

This functionality was originally available starting with SOLIDWORKS 2001Plus FCS, Revision Number 10.0 through the deprecated IModelDoc2 interface before being moved to ISketchManager in SOLIDWORKS 2010.
This method supports multibody parts. You must select the input body by calling IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 and setting its Mark argument to 1 before calling this method.
This method retrieves document property values; it does not set them. The return value is the numeric value associated with the specified user preference.
The IAssemblyDoc::IGetExplodedViewNames method is also available for working with exploded views.

The Option parameter controls whether the deleted face is refilled after deletion (in the predecessor API, a value of 1 refilled the face, 0 did not). This behavior is now controlled through the swFaceDeleteOption_e enumeration values.

The balloon note is located near the pre-selection. Use INote::GetBalloonStack with the returned note to get the IBalloonStack object, which allows control of stack properties such as direction and size, and supports adding more balloons to the stack. If the balloon style is split circle, both lower and upper text arguments are used; otherwise, only upper text is used and lower text is ignored. When text style is item number or quantity, the note text is determined through the pre-selected entity and the corresponding text argument is ignored. If the pre-selection is just a location on the drawing (not an entity), the text style should be custom and you must specify the text to use.

LowerTextStyle and LowerText are valid only when Style is set to swBS_SplitCirc. CustomSize is used only when Size is set to swBF_UserDef. QuantityPlacement values: 0 = Left, 1 = Right, 2 = Top, 3 = Bottom.
Does not support the selection of silhouette edges.

The return value (intersection count) should be used to determine the size of arrays to allocate for return values from IModelDoc2::GetRayIntersectionsPoints or IModelDoc2::IGetRayIntersectionPoints and IModelDoc2::GetRayIntersectionTopology or IModelDoc2::IGetRayIntersectionsTopology. Note that i-prefixed variants (IModelDoc2::IGetRayIntersectionPoints and IModelDoc2::IGetRayIntersectionsTopology) are also available as alternatives to the non-i-prefixed methods for retrieving intersection results.
When CapEnds is enabled for bidirectional offsets, the ends are capped with arcs.

Point 2 defines the bottom line from point 1, and point 3 is diagonal to point 1. The deprecated function included a boolean parameter to control whether automatic constraints were added to the rectangle geometry; this control is not available in the replacement method.
All coordinate values (X1, Y1, Z1, X2, Y2, Z2) are in meters.

The selected sketch segment must be a closed entity, meaning its start and end points are the same (for example, a complete circle). The two specified points determine where the closed segment is split into two pieces.
The selected sketch segment must be an open entity; for example, the start and end points cannot be the same.
Do not use this method to copy assemblies, drawings, or parts with in-context references. Instead, use ISldWorks::CopyDocument or ISldWorks::ICopyDocument.

The predecessor function IModelDoc2::SketchPolygon left the PropertyManager page in edit mode after polygon creation, requiring IModelDoc2::SetPickMode, IModelDoc2::ClearSelection2, or exiting the sketch to complete the operation.
The method returns a double array (System.double[]), despite the System.object return type in the signature.
The ParamsIn parameter should contain three arrays: a properties array (integers), a knots array (doubles), and a control point coordinates array (doubles).

The deprecated ApplyTexture method accepted individual parameters for Scale (int), Angle (double), TextureFilename (string), and BlendColor (bool). These settings should now be configured as properties of the Texture object passed to SetTexture.
Unlike the obsoleted NeedsRebuild property, this property recognizes frozen features when determining rebuild status.
Call this method before calling IModelDocExtension::GetRenderMaterials2 to determine the size of the array needed for that method.

Examples demonstrating usage are available: Create Solid Bodies Using Geometry and Topology (C#), Create Solid Bodies Using Geometry and Topology (VB.NET), and Create Solid Bodies Using Geometry and Topology (VBA).

Each body must be transformed in the coordinate space of the top-level assembly so that it is positioned the same with respect to the other bodies as it is in assembly space. To align the bodies, apply the transformation from IComponent2::Transform2 using IBody2::ApplyTransform.
Only solid bodies should be used for mass property calculations. Surface bodies are not valid inputs.
The coordinate system for moment calculations defaults to the document's origin if not explicitly set using IMassProperty::SetCoordinateSystem (or equivalent method on IMassProperty2).
This method returns recalculated up-to-date mass property values regardless of the document's NeedsRebuild status.

If no bodies or components are specified (via SelectedItems or other selection methods), mass properties calculations automatically include all available solid bodies in the document. For parts, this means all solid bodies. For assemblies, this includes all bodies in all components.
Temporary bodies can be included in mass property calculations if supported by the IMassProperty2 interface's body specification methods.
The Callout property of the ISelectData object is not used by this method.

When boxHeight is 0, the created sheet body has dimensions boxWidth x boxLength. Additionally, when boxAxis is not parallel to the Z axis (0,0,1), the orientation of boxWidth and boxLength is not defined.
QuantityPlacement numeric values: 0 = Left, 1 = Right, 2 = Top, 3 = Bottom
Use .STP for the file name extension when exporting to STEP 242 format.
The DetailedCutList parameter is valid only if BomType is set to swBomType_e.swBomType_Indented.
For additional information about the output format, see Parasolid's documentation of PK_BODY_make_curves_outline.
Tolerance values are returned in meters.
Example available: Cut Body in Half Using Macro Feature (VBA)
Return value: True if successful, false if not.
See also INote::IsBendLineNote Property to determine if a note is a bend line note before retrieving its values.

You can obtain the ProgID or CLSID strings using a combination of the Microsoft OLE/COM Object Viewer and the registry editor. Both ProgID and CLSID are equivalent ways to refer to the same ActiveX control.

The method works regardless of whether the underlying component document is a part, an assembly, or a subassembly. Additional VBA examples for note-specific use cases: Get Corresponding Note in Assembly and Get Corresponding Note in Part.

This method replaces the deprecated BendAllowance property, which previously returned a System.double value. The bend allowance value is now accessible through the CustomBendAllowance object returned by this method.

This method replaces the deprecated BendAllowanceType property, which previously returned a System.int value indicating the type of bend allowance. The bend allowance type is now accessible through the CustomBendAllowance object returned by this method.

This method replaces the deprecated BendTableFile property, which previously returned a System.string containing the file path to the bend table. The bend table file information is now accessible through the CustomBendAllowance object returned by this method.

This method replaces the deprecated KFactor property, which previously returned a System.double value. The K-factor value is now accessible through the CustomBendAllowance object returned by this method.

This method is particularly useful when your PropertyManager page has selection lists, for example when selecting edges to fillet. After enabling a cursor, you typically should not disable it manually; instead, let SOLIDWORKS determine when to disable it. SOLIDWORKS automatically disables the cursor when the cursor moves even slightly and replaces it with the cursor appropriate for the operation in progress.
The returned IFeature interface gives you access to the feature APIs such as IFeature::Name to get or set the name of the inserted feature.
The parametric axis (U or V) is specified using constants swSplitFaceOnParamU or swSplitFaceOnParamV when defining the split with IModeler::ISplitFaceOnParamCount2.
The returned object is a PropertyManagerPageControl typed as System.object for compatibility.
The return value is specifically a PropertyManagerPageControl object, though it is returned as System.object.
The return value is specifically of type PropertyManagerPageControl, not a generic object.
This property corresponds to selecting From File as the surface finish type on the Surface Finish tab of the Appearances PropertyManager page and browsing to an image.
The Name parameter should specify a .sldprt file extension for the part document.
The CustomBendAllowance parameter encapsulates the bend allowance value, which was previously set as a double via the BendAllowance property.
The CustomBendAllowance parameter encapsulates the bend allowance type, which was previously set as an integer via the BendAllowanceType property.
The CustomBendAllowance parameter encapsulates the bend table file path, which was previously set as a string via the BendTableFile property.
The CustomBendAllowance parameter encapsulates the K-factor value, which was previously set as a double via the KFactor property.
Usage examples are available: Change Bend Radius of Sheet Metal Part (VBA) and Get Fixed Face of Sheet Metal Part (VBA).
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a bend allowance value property of type double.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a bend allowance type property of type int.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a bend table file path property of type string.
The returned CustomBendAllowance object includes a K-factor property of type double.

The UseAnchorPoint parameter requires that the appropriate sheet format anchor point exists on the sheet; if it does not exist when UseAnchorPoint is true, the method may not use the anchor positioning. The return value is null specifically when a revision table already exists on the sheet (not on other sheets in the drawing).

SelectByID2 differs from AddSelectionListObject and AddSelectionListObjects in that it programmatically preselects objects in the user interface before adding them to the selection list, whereas the other methods add objects without preselecting them in the UI.
For linear springs, the end point parameters (PDirDisp1 and PDirDisp2) can be a linear edge, vertex, or sketch point.
End point parameters (PDirDisp1, PDirDisp2) can accept linear edges, vertices, sketch segments, or sketch points.
This method returns the face in the space of the sketch or drawing view.

The returned ISplineParamData contains parameterization information including control points in the sketch coordinate system. For access to individual ISketchSegment and ISketchSpline objects, see ISketch::GetSketchSegments or ISketch::IEnumSketchSegments.

In the predecessor method SketchReplace (SOLIDWORKS 2014), when MakeConstruction was false, the replaced sketch entity was retained as regular geometry rather than deleted. In SketchReplace2, false deletes the replaced entity.
The mark value, which was previously passed as an integer parameter in the superseded Select method, is now controlled through the Data parameter's SelectData object.

When EntitiesToFullyDefine is false (to dimension only selected entities), the sketch entities must be pre-selected using IModelDocExtension::SelectByID2 with an appropriate mark value before calling this method.
It is an error to supply only one datum. Both horizontal and vertical datums must be provided together, or neither should be supplied.

The return value indicates success or failure status. In the predecessor method, swAutodimStatusSuccess indicated successful dimensioning, and swAutodimStatus_e enumeration provided values for possible failure reasons such as swAutodimStatus_DatumNotSupplied.

For XOffset and YOffset parameters: In an equation such as y = sin(x) [x=0 to 2Pi], the XOffset moves the start point (0,0) by XOffset in the x direction, and the YOffset moves the start point (0,0) by YOffset in the y direction.
For a usage example, see the VBA example 'Change Bend Radius of Sketched Bend', which demonstrates how to access selections for modifying a sketched bend feature.

RangeStart and RangeEnd must be double values; string values are not supported. XOffset moves the start point in the x direction (e.g., in y = sin(x) [x=0 to 2Pi], the start point (0,0) is moved by XOffset in x). YOffset moves the start point in the y direction (e.g., in y = sin(x) [x=0 to 2Pi], the start point (0,0) is moved by YOffset in y).

RangeStart and RangeEnd must be specified as double values, not string values. XOffset moves the start point (where the parameter equals 0) in the x direction. YOffset moves the start point (where the parameter equals 0) in the y direction. For example, in the equation y = sin(x) with range x=0 to 2Pi, the start point (0,0) is shifted by XOffset and YOffset in their respective directions.
The superseded GetFocalPoint method returned a double value, whereas GetFocalPoint2 returns a SketchPoint object representing the focal point.

Example output formats: BaseVersion returns values like 'SW2009_a1' (SW + year + underscore + version identifier), and CurrentVersion returns values like 'd080407.062' (date-based build identifier with dot and sub-number).
If you specify a HintString, it must be preceded by a comma.
The TemplateName parameter requires a fully qualified path and name of the template, not just the template name alone.

The returned ISurfaceParameterizationData object encapsulates the parameterization data that was previously returned as an array of 11 doubles: uRange[2] (low and high range for U), vRange[2] (low and high range for V), uBoundType (two integers packed into a double describing behavior at start and end of U range), vBoundType (two integers packed into a double describing behavior at start and end of V range), uProps[2] (four integers packed into two doubles representing U parameterization properties), vProps[2] (four integers packed into two doubles representing V parameterization properties), and numProps (two integers packed into a double representing the number of properties in uProps and vProps). Boundary type values include: 13733 (Infinite), 13734 (Extendable), 13735 (Not Extendable), 13701 (Periodic), 13736 (Periodic but not continuously differentiable across boundary), and 13741 (Degenerate). Parameterization property values include: 13701 (Periodic), 13737 (All derivatives continuous), 13738 (All derivatives not necessarily continuous), 13739 (Linear), 13740 (Circular), and 13746 (Bounds at ends of parameter range are coincident). A parameterization can only degenerate at the end of its range unless the surface is a type of Bsurface. For periodic surfaces (cylinder, cone, torus, sphere), U is the periodic direction. The -10000 to 10000 parameter range is the modeling limit for the modeler, representing effectively infinite size. To get the true parameter range that a face is using on the surface, use IFace2::GetBox, ISurface::GetClosestPointOn, and ISurface::ReverseEvaluate.

If your application uses this method, curves created by IModeler::ICreateArc or IModeler::ICreateLine (the I-prefixed variants) must also be trimmed using ICurve::CreateTrimmedCurve2 before being passed to this method.

See code examples: Get Table-driven Pattern Properties (VBA), Get Points of Repeating Elements in Table-driven Pattern (C#), Get Points of Repeating Elements in Table-driven Patterns (VB.NET), Get Points of Repeating Elements in Table-driven Pattern (VBA).
Call this method before calling ISurfaceOffsetFeatureData::IGetEntities to determine the required array size.
Button images can be either BMP (bitmap) or PNG file formats. The original AddCustomButton supported 8-, 16-, 24-, or 32-bit depth images.

Related methods for cell text manipulation: ITableAnnotation::GetCellTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::SetCellTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::GetTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::SetTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::GetCellUseDocTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::GetUseDocTextFormat, ITableAnnotation::IsCellTextEditable. Related properties for cell text alignment: ITableAnnotation::CellTextHorizontalJustification, ITableAnnotation::CellTextVerticalJustification.

The deprecated GetErrorList method returned all error arrays as System.object types and was callable from C# and other .NET languages. Unlike its replacement IGetErrorList2, GetErrorList provided .NET language support. Users migrating from GetErrorList should note that IGetErrorList2 is only supported in in-process, unmanaged C++ and is not available in VBA, VB.NET, C#, or C++/CLI.
The return value is of type MultiJogLeader, though declared as System.object in the signature.
Although the method signature returns System.object, the actual return value is an array of doubles that should be cast appropriately to access the arc data.

The data kept with the IDisplayData object is strictly for display purposes and allows you to recreate unintelligent geometry. It does not provide associations or detailed information about the geometry.

Although the return type is declared as System.object, the actual runtime type is a System.double array. Related methods for working with dimension data include IView::GetDimensionCount4, IView::GetDimensionDisplayInfo5, IView::GetDimensionDisplayInfoSize2, IView::GetDimensionDisplayString4, IView::GetDimensionIds4, IView::GetDimensionString4, IView::GetFirstDisplayDimension5, and the IView::ProjectedDimensions property.

When attempting to change a BOM table column to Quantity type, this method returns a failure code because the operation is not supported. When changing a column to custom property type, the column title is set to empty and must be explicitly set afterward using ITableAnnotation::SetColumnTitle2.
Returns the first display dimension in the view. If no display dimensions exist, returns null.
The return value is a BreakLine object (despite the method signature indicating System.object).

Once a drawing view has precision quality (Faceted=false), it cannot be changed back to draft quality (Faceted=true). However, geometry can still be displayed in draft quality if user preferences indicate new drawing views should be created that way.
To display a drawing view shaded with edges, set the system option swDrawingsDefaultDisplayTypeHLREdgesWhenShaded to True and set Mode to swSHADED.
For inserting a BOM using Microsoft Excel, you can also use IView::IInsertBomTable in addition to IView::InsertBomTable.
